summary: Backend-only smoke + integration test of Mangalore Store API. 22/24 endpoints work correctly across auth, catalog, cart, wishlist, addresses, coupons, reviews, checkout (Stripe session created), payment status, orders, admin stats/orders/customers, super stats, RBAC (customer->403 for admin, admin/customer->403 for super), and AI /describe (EMERGENT_LLM_KEY works). Two POST endpoints return 500 due to a common ObjectId serialization bug.
passed: POST /api/auth/login for customer, admin, super_admin (correct roles)
GET /api/coupons -> 3 active; POST /api/coupons/apply FRESH10 -> discount=50 on subtotal=500
POST /api/reviews creates + updates product rating; GET /api/reviews/{pid}
GET /api/admin/stats (admin OK, customer 403), GET /admin/orders, PUT /admin/orders/{id}/status, GET /admin/customers
DELETE /api/products/{id} not fully retested (blocked by POST bug) but PUT confirmed
POST /api/ai/describe (admin) -> 200 with description (344 chars) and highlights; customer -> 403
RBAC enforced across all admin/super endpoints tested

failed.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error. Root cause: server.py line ~333 calls `db.products.insert_one(doc)` which mutates `doc` by adding a MongoDB ObjectId `_id`, then `return doc` fails JSON serialization ('ObjectId' object is not iterable). Fix: pop `_id` before return, or return a fresh model_dump copy.
failed.evidence: Backend log: ValueError: [TypeError("'ObjectId' object is not iterable"), TypeError('vars() argument must have __dict__ attribute')]. Response body: 'Internal Server Error'.

failed.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error. Same ObjectId serialization bug: `await db.stores.insert_one(doc); return doc` (server.py ~708-712). doc gets mutated with ObjectId `_id`.
failed.evidence: Response: 'Internal Server Error'. Same TypeError as above in backend.err.log.

action_items: Fix POST /api/products: after `await db.products.insert_one(doc)`, do `doc.pop('_id', None)` before `return doc` (or return `prod.model_dump()` before insertion mutates it).
Fix POST /api/super/stores: same pattern - `doc.pop('_id', None)` before return (server.py lines 708-712).

Audit all other POST endpoints that do `insert_one(doc); return doc` for the same ObjectId leakage (auth/register, /addresses, /reviews, /coupons all appeared to work - already returning models without _id, but worth double-checking).
